description:
You are a junior data-analyst who has just been handed a small HTML page that was
    captured with a headless browser.  
    Your job is to “re-scrape” that page from disk, turn the one HTML table it
    contains into a clean CSV file, and produce a one-line text
    summary of the quarterly total.  Everything must be done from an interactive
    Linux terminal session; you will not be given root privileges.

    Existing material
    -----------------
    1. The HTML file to be scraped already exists at  
       /home/user/projects/scrape/input/page.html

    2. The HTML contains one table whose id attribute is “sales”.  
       Columns (in order) are:  
          – Region  
          – Sales (string, e.g. “$12,400”)  
          – Quarter (e.g. “Q1”)  

    Goal #1 – Create the CSV
    ------------------------
    • Read /home/user/projects/scrape/input/page.html with whatever non-GUI,
      headless-friendly tooling you like (curl, python, lynx ‑dump, etc.).  
    • Strip the “$” sign and the “,” thousands separator from the Sales values so
      they become plain integers.  
    • Save the resulting comma-separated table (including the header row) to  

          /home/user/projects/scrape/output/sales_q1.csv

      The file MUST match this exact layout (including the trailing newline):

          Region,Sales,Quarter
          North,12400,Q1
          South,11300,Q1
          East,10900,Q1
          West,9800,Q1

    Goal #2 – Produce a summary log
    -------------------------------
    • Compute the grand total of the numeric Sales column in the new CSV.  
    • Write that single integer to a text file located at  

          /home/user/projects/scrape/output/summary.log

      The file must contain exactly one line, formatted precisely as:

          TOTAL_SALES_Q1=44400

      followed by a single newline character.

    Important verification notes
    ----------------------------
    • The automated test harness will check only the two files mentioned above.
    • Directory /home/user/projects/scrape/output/ may not exist; create it if
      needed with standard user permissions (0700 or 0755 are both fine).
    • Do not add BOM markers, extra spaces, or blank lines to either file.
    • Use at most 3–4 shell commands (pipelines are fine) to accomplish the task.
truth:
Quarterly Sales

Quarter 1 Sales

RegionSalesQuarter
North$12,400Q1
South$11,300Q1
East$10,900Q1
West$9,800Q1
]]>
